text: by Dr. Matt Qvortrup with a team of international experts on law and politics Referendums Around the World is the definitive book on why and when referendums are held on specific policy issues. From 1793 to the present, this comprehensive study details the history and development of referendums from Europe to the Americas, Asia, Africa and Oceania, documenting the long-standing practices in countries like Switzerland and the United States as well as the rapid expansion of ballot measures in Latin America, Africa and Asia. This updated edition will remain the standard in the field and is a must-read for scholars and students interested in the workings of popular sovereignty around the world.
